seoTitle: Get an Access Token in HubSpot: Generate Guide
metaDescription: Generate a secure HubSpot access token through a Private App, choosing object scopes and revealing your bearer string for API authentication.
intro: Since HubSpot moved away from legacy API keys, generating an access token now means creating a Private App and choosing which objects it can read or write. This guide covers setting up that app, selecting your scopes, and safely revealing the token string you'll use to authenticate API calls. It ends with a note on monitoring your integration through the log console.

steps.heading: Open Integrations settings
steps.body: Go to your HubSpot account settings and navigate to the Integrations directory to find Private Apps.

steps.heading: Create a Private App
steps.body: Click Create Private App and fill in your app's core metadata profile.

steps.heading: Select your scopes
steps.body: Go to the Scopes tab and check off the required read and write API access permissions.

steps.heading: Finalize the app
steps.body: Click Create App in the upper right corner to finalize the security architecture.

steps.heading: Reveal your access token
steps.body: Open the Access Token security tab and click Reveal to display your bearer token key.

steps.heading: Copy and monitor usage
steps.body: Copy the token to your clipboard and review the live API call synchronization dashboard.

faq.question: Do I still generate access tokens through legacy API keys?
faq.answer: No, HubSpot's modern security architecture requires a Private App to issue access tokens.

faq.question: What scopes should I select for my token?
faq.answer: Choose only the read and write permissions your integration actually needs, such as access to Contacts or Deals.

faq.question: Is it safe to share my access token?
faq.answer: No, it should be kept encrypted and private since it grants API access to your CRM data.

faq.question: How do I check if my token is being used correctly?
faq.answer: The live data log console lets you monitor API calls and webhook performance tied to your token.
